steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts two groups: one that “lauded this development” with “welcome transparency” (optimistic), and another that “countered” with warnings about “ideological extremes” causing “problematic” polarization (skeptical).

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “Sanguine” means optimistic and hopeful—”less sanguine” observers are those who reject optimism, fitting the countering group's skepticism exactly. The other three describe attitudes unrelated to hopefulness. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
